metadata.title: Important Calendar Dates

source.notes: Curated route-specific subset of taxpayer-facing Nebraska assessment, review, exemption, budget, and tax-bill dates. Source event IDs refer to data/app/assessment-calendar-events.json.

metadata.disclaimer: Selected dates are shown for orientation only. Confirm official filing requirements, deadlines, and instructions with the appropriate county or state office.

dates.title: Assessment Date
dates.taxpayerAction: Use this as the value-date anchor when reviewing property facts and current-year assessment context.
dates.whyItMatters: Property is generally valued as of this date before notices, levies, and tax bills follow later.

dates.title: Homestead Exemption Filing Window
dates.taxpayerAction: Eligible homeowners may file a Nebraska Homestead Exemption application with the county assessor.
dates.whyItMatters: For eligible taxpayers, the exemption can reduce property tax after values and levies are applied.

dates.title: Valuation Notice Timing
dates.taxpayerAction: Watch for a valuation-change notice if the assessed value increased or decreased.
dates.whyItMatters: A notice is the prompt to review the record, value movement, and local review timing.

dates.title: Local Valuation Review Window
dates.taxpayerAction: This is the main annual local review window for real property valuation questions.
dates.whyItMatters: This timing belongs with the County Board of Equalization calendar.

dates.title: County Board Review
dates.taxpayerAction: County Board of Equalization review, hearing, and decision timing occurs in this period.
dates.whyItMatters: This is when local review moves through the board process.

dates.title: Destroyed or Damaged Property Report Deadline
dates.taxpayerAction: Owners of significantly damaged real property may need to file a damaged real property report with the county clerk.
dates.whyItMatters: This separate process can matter when significant damage occurred before July.

dates.title: Appeal Deadline After County Board Action
dates.taxpayerAction: A taxpayer may have a later deadline to appeal final County Board action to the Tax Equalization and Review Commission.
dates.whyItMatters: This is a later review path after the county board has acted, and timing can depend on the county and action taken.

dates.title: Budget and Public Hearing Period
dates.taxpayerAction: Review public budget and hearing information from the taxing bodies that affect the property.
dates.whyItMatters: This is where certified values begin connecting to public budgets and the levy side of the bill.

dates.title: Levy Setting and Tax Request Actions
dates.taxpayerAction: Use this as the timing reference for when adopted budgets and tax requests move into final levy actions.
dates.whyItMatters: This is where the final tax-rate side of the bill comes together after assessment values are already set.

dates.title: Real and Personal Property Taxes Due
dates.taxpayerAction: Use the official county treasurer statement for payment amount, payment status, and delinquency details.
dates.whyItMatters: Tax bills follow the assessment, equalization, budget, levy, credit, and tax-list steps from earlier in the year.
